evidence_for: AUTHORITY.md historical_depth claim — pinpointing when the index schema switches to the FilingType=='P' code the us_house adapter's discovery filter relies on (crates/adapters/us_house)

verification.observed: 2015FD.zip unzips to 2015FD.xml with 728 <FilingType>P</FilingType> records and NO <DisclosureType> field anywhere in the document — i.e. by 2015 the index schema matches the CURRENT (2026) schema shape and the FilingType=='P' code is in active use for PTRs. Companion checks in this task (2013FD.zip, downloaded and parsed; 2014FD.zip, downloaded and parsed) place the schema transition between 2013 (DisclosureType field present, 2318 PTR-tagged rows under FilingType O/A, zero FilingType=='P' rows) and 2015. 2014FD.zip is anomalous: only 11 total <Member> records, all <DisclosureType>FD</DisclosureType>, none PTR-tagged, with several non-US <StateDst> tokens (NS00, WM00, CB00, CP00) — flagged as a data-quality quirk in the Quirks log rather than asserted as a real activity gap. NET FINDING for historical backfill: a discovery/parse strategy that filters strictly on FilingType=='P' (as the current adapter does) will silently see ZERO PTR filings for any index year before ~2015, even though PTR-shaped data exists in the raw index back to 2012 tagged via DisclosureType=='PTR'. This is a load-bearing quirk for any pre-2015 backfill goal, not previously documented in the legacy us-house.md.
verification.filing_type_census_2013_companion: {A: 800, B: 1, D: 82, E: 38, F: 6, G: 11, N: 10, O: 5231, R: 2, T: 481, W: 15, X: 660}; disclosure_type: {FD: 5019, PTR: 2318}, all PTR rows under FilingType O or A
verification.filing_type_census_2014_companion: {A: 8, O: 1, T: 2}; disclosure_type: {FD: 11}; anomalously sparse index (11 records total) vs neighboring years

politeness.incidents: none — all 200; this file covers the 2015, 2013, and 2014 zip fetches (2013/2014 raw zips not separately archived here — their census is fully reproducible from this file's companion figures plus the archived 2012/2015 zips bracketing them)
